binaries.title: mqac.sys (Microsoft Message Queuing AC driver) - No security-relevant change (compiler rebuild with function inlining)

changed_functions.name: ACDeviceControl (IOCTL dispatcher) — unpatched 0x14000B100, patched 0x1C000C880
key_changes: Case 0x1965108F: unpatched calls CSMAllocator::ReleaseFreeHeaps(DeviceExtension+0x140) once; patched performs three CPoolAllocator::PurgeHeaps calls on +0x10, +0x50, +0x90 directly. This is pure inlining: ReleaseFreeHeaps @ 0x1400117E0 is exactly those three PurgeHeaps calls, so both builds purge the same three offsets. No dangling reference in either build.
ProbeForRead inlined into MmUserProbeAddress bounds checks across several IOCTL paths (modernization).
Per-IOCTL buffer-size checks restructured but byte-equivalent in effect.
changed_functions.security_notes: The suspected use-after-free is not real. The unpatched single call to CSMAllocator::ReleaseFreeHeaps @ 0x1400117E0 already purges the free-heap lists at context offsets +0x10, +0x50, +0x90 (its entire body is three CPoolAllocator::PurgeHeaps calls). The patched dispatcher inlines that helper. Semantics are identical; nothing is left dangling. CPoolAllocator::PurgeHeaps releases already-free heap views (allocator maintenance), not live-object teardown.

changed_functions.name: ACPutRestoredPacket (restored-packet handler) — unpatched 0x140008AD4, patched 0x1C0009F50
key_changes: CQEntry::Buffer @ 0x14000263C inlined (returns NULL when *(entry+0x10)==0xFFFFFFFF). Both builds fold NULL into v12 = (buf+4) & -(buf!=0) and branch if (v12==0) return 0xC000009A before any dereference.
GUID compare helper replaced with RtlCompareMemory(..., 0x10).

CTransaction::RestorePacket @ 0x140021A64 inlined into the transactional tail. Its NULL check (asm 0x140021A99) and sign check (asm 0x140021AA5) already exist in the unpatched build; nothing was added.
changed_functions.security_notes: The suspected null-pointer dereference is not real. The NULL guard on the packet buffer (if (v12==0) return 0xC000009A) is present identically in both builds before any dereference. The 'added' NULL check and sign check on the transactional path are the body of CTransaction::RestorePacket @ 0x140021A64, which the patched build inlines into ACPutRestoredPacket. Both checks exist in the unpatched build.

changed_functions.name: ACCreateRemoteProxy — 0x14000644C
key_changes: ProbeForRead inlined to MmUserProbeAddress bounds check.
ExAllocatePoolWithTag replaced with ExAllocatePoolWithTagPriority(NonPagedPoolNx, ...).
Custom free wrapper replaced with direct ExFreePoolWithTag.
changed_functions.security_notes: Compiler modernization: probe inlining, NonPagedPoolNx allocation, direct ExFreePoolWithTag. No semantic security change.

changed_functions.name: ACPutRestoredPacket dispatch (see above) — covered by primary entry
key_changes: Duplicate of the ACPutRestoredPacket inlining analysis; retained for completeness.
changed_functions.security_notes: See ACPutRestoredPacket entry: inlining of CQEntry::Buffer, CQEntry::ReleaseBuffer, and CTransaction::RestorePacket. No new validation.

changed_functions.name: ACBeginGetPacket2Remote — 0x140005328
key_changes: GUID compare helper inlined to RtlCompareMemory; handler argument sourced via conditional pointer.
changed_functions.security_notes: GUID comparison inlined; no security-semantic change.

changed_functions.name: ACAssociateQueue — 0x140004E38
key_changes: ProbeForRead inlined to MmUserProbeAddress check.
Error-path free modernized: custom free replaced with ExFreePoolWithTag.
Share-access validation preserved but restructured.
changed_functions.security_notes: Compiler modernization; share-access authorization checks preserved.

changed_functions.name: ACCreateQueue — 0x140005F20
key_changes: ProbeForRead calls inlined to MmUserProbeAddress bounds checks.
Index-lookup helper inlined with NULL/zero guard present in both builds.
ExAllocatePool modernized to ExAllocatePoolWithTagPriority(NonPagedPoolNx).
changed_functions.security_notes: Probe inlining and pool modernization; the index guard exists in both builds. No new security-relevant behavior.

changed_functions.name: CUserHeader constructor — 0x1400138D8
key_changes: Subtraction-based pointer comparison rewritten as direct equality comparison of the same fields.

changed_functions.name: ACSendMessage — 0x140009478
key_changes: ProbeForRead inlined to MmUserProbeAddress check.
Local zero-fill is memset(..., 0x118) in BOTH builds (identical size; no 0x110->0x118 change and no added +0x78 zero-init).
changed_functions.security_notes: Probe inlining and helper inlining. The previously suspected uninitialized-memory zero-init addition is not present; the memset size is identical (0x118) in both builds.

changed_functions.name: ACPutRemotePacket — 0x140008950
key_changes: Device-context resolution restructured before lookup.
Refcount decrement helper inlined (*(ctx+0x94) -= 1).
Added WPP trace of refcount value after decrement.
changed_functions.security_notes: Helper inlining and refcount tracing; no semantic security change.

downgraded_findings.reason: Compiler inlining, not a fix. The unpatched case 0x1965108F calls CSMAllocator::ReleaseFreeHeaps @ 0x1400117E0, whose entire body is three CPoolAllocator::PurgeHeaps calls on context offsets +0x10, +0x50, +0x90. The patched dispatcher inlines those three calls. Both builds purge the same three offsets, so no list entry or sub-context is left dangling and there is no use-after-free. CPoolAllocator::PurgeHeaps releases already-free heap views, not live objects.

Compiler inlining, not a fix. CQEntry::Buffer @ 0x14000263C returns NULL when the entry handle field is 0xFFFFFFFF; both builds guard it with if (v12==0) return 0xC000009A before any dereference. The transactional NULL check and sign check that appeared to be added are the body of CTransaction::RestorePacket @ 0x140021A64 (NULL check at asm 0x140021A99, sign check at asm 0x140021AA5), which the patched build inlines into ACPutRestoredPacket. Both checks are present in the unpatched build; no dereference of a NULL result occurs in either build.

summary: No security-relevant change was found in mqac.sys between the two builds. Both behaviors originally suspected as vulnerabilities are compiler-inlining artifacts. (1) The suspected use-after-free in the IOCTL dispatcher (ACDeviceControl, case 0x1965108F): the unpatched single call CSMAllocator::ReleaseFreeHeaps @ 0x1400117E0 already purges the free-heap lists at context offsets +0x10, +0x50, +0x90 (its body is three CPoolAllocator::PurgeHeaps calls); the patched dispatcher inlines those three calls. Identical semantics, no dangling reference. (2) The suspected null-pointer dereference in ACPutRestoredPacket: the packet-buffer NULL guard (if (v12==0) return 0xC000009A) is present in both builds before any dereference, and the transactional NULL/sign checks are the body of CTransaction::RestorePacket @ 0x140021A64 (checks at asm 0x140021A99 and 0x140021AA5), which the patched build inlines. Remaining driver-wide differences are toolchain churn: WIL feature-staging code, renamed WPP trace macros, alternate memset intrinsics, Control Flow Guard dispatch thunks, ProbeForRead inlining, and NonPagedPoolNx allocation modernization. No omitted security-relevant change was identified across the changed functions.
